chunk_summary: Obesity severity bands and lifestyle intensity thresholds guide therapy escalation in Indian children, emphasizing structured interventions and early action for class 2–3 obesity to mitigate cardiometabolic risks.
learning_objectives: Understand obesity severity bands and their adult-equivalent BMI cutoffs for Indian children aged 5–18 years.
Learn the minimum requirements for intensive health behavior and lifestyle treatment (IHBLT) in pediatric obesity care.
Recognize the importance of waist circumference and its role in predicting MASLD and dysglycemia risk.
Identify criteria for therapy escalation based on obesity class and metabolic markers.
key_facts: Class 2 obesity is defined as 120% to <140% of the adult-equivalent BMI of 27; class 3 obesity is ≥140%.
Clinically meaningful IHBLT requires ~26 hours of contact over 3–12 months, with optimal results at ≥52 hours.
A practical minimum for IHBLT in Indian settings is 4–6 structured contacts within 12 weeks, including family sessions.
Waist circumference should be recorded alongside BMI to assess central adiposity and related risks like MASLD.
Escalate therapy intensity for class 2–3 obesity or worsening ALT, BP, glycemia, or lipids despite initial advice.
reference_quote: Evidence-based obesity care shows a dose-response relationship for intensive health behavior and lifestyle treatment (IHBLT), with clinically meaningful benefit typically requiring at least ~26 hours of contact over 3–12 months and greater consistency at ≥52 hours. (Reference 2)
document_key_topics: Obesity severity bands and BMI cutoffs for Indian children
Lifestyle therapy intensity thresholds and structured interventions
Role of waist circumference in predicting metabolic risks
Criteria for therapy escalation in pediatric obesity care
